text.title: interface-analysis Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for interface-analysis as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Specifying only the happy-path flow without error scenarios.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Real integrations fail; undocumented error handling produces ad-hoc implementation decisions at development time that are inconsistent across interfaces and invisible to operations teams.

failure-mode.description: Omitting volume and frequency estimates from the interface specification.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Systems cannot handle unexpected load; volume underestimates are the leading cause of production performance failures at integration points.

failure-mode.description: Specifying authentication and security as "TBD" or "standard security".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Security requirements left unspecified default to the developer's judgment, producing inconsistent auth mechanisms, missing encryption, and audit failures.

failure-mode.description: Interface versioning not specified at initial design time.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Unversioned interfaces break downstream consumers on every change with no migration path; versioning strategy must be decided before the interface is published.

failure-mode.description: Inventory rot: an interface register collected by spreadsheet ages within one quarter; without an automated feed (CMDB + API gateway logs + iPaaS catalogue) the doc becomes a liability.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Automate the discovery pass quarterly; diff against the committed register to surface drift. Make the audit part of the SDD cycle.

failure-mode.description: Shadow integrations (Zapier, Make, manual SFTP scripts on someone's laptop, Excel macros pulling from SharePoint) never make it onto the BA's diagram and cause every audit finding.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Explicitly task a separate discovery pass against email/Slack/Workato/Power Automate logs to surface candidates. Make shadow IT discovery a sub-task.

failure-mode.description: Capability-to-system mapping disagrees across Architecture, Finance, and IT — the BA inherits the political fight, not just the modelling task.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Get architecture sign-off on the capability-to-system map before launching the landscape discovery. Store the mapping as a separate approved artifact.

failure-mode.description: Governance theatre: contract review boards approve in hours what teams need to ship in minutes; ungoverned channels appear (slack DMs, untracked dev keys).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Encode governance rules in CI (Spectral, oasdiff) so teams get instant feedback; escalate only genuine exceptions to a review board.

failure-mode.description: PII/data-classification is best-guessed at landscape level; field-level truth lives in the per-interface spec, so the landscape view is structurally lossy.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pull sensitivity classification from the data catalogue (Purview/Collibra/Atlan), not from interface specs. Mark unknowns explicitly.

failure-mode.description: LLM agents over-collapse heterogeneous flows ("all integrations are REST") — async/file/EDI/SWIFT/HL7 v2/EDIFACT channels are routinely misclassified.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require the agent to cite a payload sample or gateway log line per row. Preserve the protocol family in a separate column for diversity.

failure-mode.description: Agents conflate "interface" with "endpoint" — one interface can have many endpoints; force the prompt to use IF-ID = logical contract, not URL path.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Define interface explicitly in the prompt: "one logical contract between source and target; one row per source-target pair; multiple endpoints under one interface are subsumed."

failure-mode.description: Direction perspective drift: across a portfolio, "inbound" depends on which system you sit at. Anchor each row to source→target, never relative terms.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Enforce source→target direction in every row. Use consistent naming (Source, Target, not Upstream/Downstream).

failure-mode.description: Channel misclassification (REST vs RPC vs Webhook vs Event) — require the agent to cite a payload sample or gateway log line per row.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require evidence: each row has a `payload_sample_link` or `gateway_log_ref`. Reject rows without evidence.

failure-mode.description: Capability mapping invented from system names: agents hallucinate capability codes; require a closed list (the org's capability map) as input.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pass the capability map to the agent as a closed vocabulary. Reject any capability code not in the list.

failure-mode.description: Sensitivity classification: agents default to "internal" when unsure — instead instruct them to emit `unknown` and surface for human review.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Forbid the agent from guessing; mandate `unknown` for any row without explicit classification in the source system.

failure-mode.description: Owner fields auto-filled from system name pattern guesses; only accept owners present in CMDB output.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Validate every owner against the CMDB extract. Reject agents' guesses; flag as `tbd` and escalate to human.

failure-mode.description: Criticality is a business judgement, not a technical metric — never let the agent set tier-1 without explicit human sign-off.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Forbid the agent from setting criticality > 2 without explicit approval. Tier-1 requires business owner sign-off.

failure-mode.description: Long-tail integrations (EDI X12, SWIFT, HL7 v2, EDIFACT, fixed-width files) get squashed into "file transfer" — preserve the protocol family in a separate column.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Keep protocol, channel, and format as separate columns. Preserve heterogeneity.

failure-mode.description: Shadow IT: agents will not find what is not in their inputs. Explicitly task a separate pass against email/Slack/Workato/Power Automate to surface candidates.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Shadow IT discovery is a separate pass with human spot-checking. Do not expect agents to find what is hidden.
